# Bee Collection 2B — Illustrated Art Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Expand the Bee Collection registry to the agreed 30 bees and replace the tier-emoji placeholders with illustrated cartoon-mascot art, generated by a build tool (cloned from the background pipeline) and lazy-loaded in the album with an emoji fallback.

**Architecture:** Pure-logic changes (registry + 5 new milestone predicates) go in `bee-core.js` (TDD). The DOM layer (`bee-collection.js`) gains an art-manifest loader and a `beeArt()` helper that renders `<img>` when art exists and falls back to emoji otherwise. A new `tools/generate-bees.js` (clone of `generate-backgrounds.js`) turns `tools/bee-prompts.json` into `wwwroot/images/bees/*.webp` + a manifest; the user runs it. Everything is built and testable on placeholders first — images are the last step.

**Tech Stack:** Vanilla browser JS, Node 22 (`node --test`), `@fal-ai/client` + `sharp` (already deps) for the generator.

**Spec:** `docs/plans/2026-06-01-bee-collection-2b-art-design.md`

---

## File Structure

**Create:**
- `tools/bee-prompts.json` — 30 entries `{ id: { desc, palette } }`.
- `tools/generate-bees.js` — fal.ai Flux → sharp webp → manifest (clone of `generate-backgrounds.js`).
- `test/bee-art.test.js` — drift test (registry ids ↔ prompt keys) + prompt shape.
- (User-generated) `WordPlay/wwwroot/images/bees/<id>.webp` ×30 + `bees-manifest.json`.

**Modify:**
- `WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-core.js` — `BEES` 8 → 30; `commonIds()`; 5 new `MILESTONE_PREDICATES`.
- `test/bee-core.test.js` — update the registry test (length 30 + tier counts), add predicate + `commonIds` tests.
- `WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-collection.js` — extend milestone `context`; add `_beeArtManifest` + `loadBeeArtManifest` + `beeArt` + `beeArtFallback`; swap the 3 art spots; manifest-load + re-render in `renderHive`.
- `WordPlay/wwwroot/css/app.css` — `.bee-art-img` / `.bee-art-emoji` sizing.
- `WordPlay/wwwroot/index.html`, `WordPlay/wwwroot/sw.js`, `WordPlay/wwwroot/js/app.js` — version markers.
- `README.md` — update the Hive section.

**Conventions:** no `Co-Authored-By`; run from repo root `D:\Projects\Repos\WordPlay`; single test file `node --test test/<file>`; full suite `npm test`.

---

## Task 1: Expand the registry to 30 bees + `commonIds`

**Files:**
- Modify: `WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-core.js`
- Modify: `test/bee-core.test.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the registry test.** In `test/bee-core.test.js`, find the first test (currently `"BEES registry has the 8 starter bees with required fields"` asserting `core.BEES.length === 8`). Replace that single test with:

```js
test("BEES registry has 30 bees with required fields", () => {
  assert.strictEqual(core.BEES.length, 30);
  const ids = new Set();
  for (const b of core.BEES) {
    assert.ok(b.id && typeof b.id === "string");
    assert.ok(!ids.has(b.id), "duplicate id " + b.id);
    ids.add(b.id);
    assert.ok(b.name && typeof b.name === "string");
    assert.ok(["common","uncommon","rare","epic","legendary"].includes(b.tier));
    assert.ok(["coinPerWord","honeyPerGoal","honeycombCoins","dailyHint"].includes(b.perk.type));
    assert.ok(Number.isFinite(b.perk.value));
    assert.ok(typeof b.flavor === "string" && b.flavor.length > 0);
    assert.ok(b.source === "discovery" || b.source.startsWith("milestone:"));
  }
});

test("registry tier counts are 8/8/7/4/3", () => {
  const c = {};
  for (const b of core.BEES) c[b.tier] = (c[b.tier] || 0) + 1;
  assert.deepStrictEqual(c, { common: 8, uncommon: 8, rare: 7, epic: 4, legendary: 3 });
});

test("commonIds returns exactly the 8 common ids", () => {
  const ids = core.commonIds();
  assert.strictEqual(ids.length, 8);
  for (const id of ids) assert.strictEqual(core.getBee(id).tier, "common");
});
```

(Leave the other existing tests — `getBee`, `MAX_ACTIVE` — unchanged.)

## Task 2: Five new milestone predicates

**Files:**
- Modify: `WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-core.js`
- Modify: `test/bee-core.test.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add failing tests** — append to `test/bee-core.test.js`:

```js
test("new milestone predicates evaluate at their boundaries", () => {
  const base = { ownedIds: [] };
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ...base, dailyStreak: 7 }), ["warden"]);
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ...base, dailyStreak: 6 }), []);
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ...base, difficultyTier: 2 }), ["artisan"]);
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ...base, difficultyTier: 4 }).sort(), ["artisan", "solstice"]);
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ...base, highestLevel: 500 }), ["luminary"]);
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ...base, highestLevel: 499 }), []);
});

test("allCommons milestone unlocks empress only when all 8 commons owned", () => {
  const commons = core.commonIds();
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ownedIds: commons }), ["empress"]);
  // Missing one common -> not unlocked
  assert.deepStrictEqual(core.evaluateMilestones({ ownedIds: commons.slice(1) }), []);
});

test("evaluateMilestones returns all 7 milestone bees for a maxed-out player", () => {
  const ctx = {
    ownedIds: core.commonIds(), // owns all commons (satisfies allCommons) but no milestone bees yet
    honeycombRankIndex: 6, questsCompleted: 1,
    dailyStreak: 7, difficultyTier: 4, highestLevel: 500
  };
  // All 7 milestone bees unlock at once (empress included via allCommons):
  assert.deepStrictEqual(
    core.evaluateMilestones(ctx).sort(),
    ["artisan", "empress", "luminary", "monarch", "regent", "solstice", "warden"]);
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run to verify failure**

Run: `node --test test/bee-core.test.js`
Expected: FAIL — `warden`/`artisan`/etc. not returned (predicates missing), and the Task-1 "every milestone key has a predicate" test also failing.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the 5 predicates.** In `bee-core.js`, extend `MILESTONE_PREDICATES` (keep `honeycombQueen` and `questComplete`):

```js
  var MILESTONE_PREDICATES = {
    honeycombQueen: function (ctx) { return (ctx.honeycombRankIndex || 0) >= 6; },
    questComplete:  function (ctx) { return (ctx.questsCompleted || 0) >= 1; },
    dailyStreak7:   function (ctx) { return (ctx.dailyStreak || 0) >= 7; },
    tierHard:       function (ctx) { return (ctx.difficultyTier || 0) >= 2; },
    tierMaster:     function (ctx) { return (ctx.difficultyTier || 0) >= 4; },
    reachLevel500:  function (ctx) { return (ctx.highestLevel || 0) >= 500; },
    allCommons:     function (ctx) {
      var owned = ctx.ownedIds || [];
      var commons = commonIds();
      for (var i = 0; i < commons.length; i++) if (owned.indexOf(commons[i]) === -1) return false;
      return true;
    }
  };
```

(`MILESTONE_KEYS = Object.keys(MILESTONE_PREDICATES)` is already derived right below this object — no change needed there.)

- [ ] **Step 4: Run to verify pass**

Run: `node --test test/bee-core.test.js`
Expected: PASS (all, including the registry "every milestone key has a predicate" test). Then run `npm test` — all green.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-core.js test/bee-core.test.js
git commit -m "feat: add five new bee milestone predicates"
```

## Task 4: Art manifest loader + `beeArt` rendering swap (`bee-collection.js`)

**Files:**
- Modify: `WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-collection.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the manifest loader + `beeArt` helpers.** Insert these near `beeTierEmoji`/`beeTierColor` (e.g. directly after `beeTierColor`):

```js
// Lazily-loaded set of bee ids that have illustrated art. null until first load.
let _beeArtManifest = null;

function loadBeeArtManifest() {
    if (_beeArtManifest) return Promise.resolve(_beeArtManifest);
    return fetch("images/bees/bees-manifest.json")
        .then(r => (r.ok ? r.json() : []))
        .then(ids => { _beeArtManifest = new Set(Array.isArray(ids) ? ids : []); return _beeArtManifest; })
        .catch(() => { _beeArtManifest = new Set(); return _beeArtManifest; });
}

// Returns illustrated <img> art for a bee if available, else the tier emoji span.
function beeArt(bee, cls) {
    if (_beeArtManifest && _beeArtManifest.has(bee.id)) {
        return '<img class="bee-art-img ' + (cls || "") + '" src="images/bees/' + bee.id
            + '.webp" alt="' + bee.name + '" onerror="beeArtFallback(this, \'' + bee.tier + '\')">';
    }
    return '<span class="bee-art-emoji">' + beeTierEmoji(bee.tier) + '</span>';
}

// onerror handler: replace a broken art <img> with the tier emoji.
function beeArtFallback(img, tier) {
    const span = document.createElement("span");
    span.className = "bee-art-emoji";
    span.textContent = beeTierEmoji(tier);
    if (img && img.parentNode) img.parentNode.replaceChild(span, img);
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Trigger manifest load + re-render in `renderHive`.** In `renderHive`, immediately after the `ensureHive();` call near the top, add:

```js
    if (!_beeArtManifest) {
        loadBeeArtManifest().then(() => { if (state.showHive) renderHive(); });
    }
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Swap the three art spots.** In `renderHive`:

  (a) Equip slot — replace:
```js
            ? `<button class="hive-slot filled" data-unequip="${id}" style="border-color:${beeTierColor(HiveCore.getBee(id).tier)}"><span class="hive-slot-emoji">${beeTierEmoji(HiveCore.getBee(id).tier)}</span><span class="hive-slot-name">${HiveCore.getBee(id).name}</span></button>`
```
  with:
```js
            ? `<button class="hive-slot filled" data-unequip="${id}" style="border-color:${beeTierColor(HiveCore.getBee(id).tier)}"><span class="hive-slot-emoji">${beeArt(HiveCore.getBee(id), "bee-art-slot")}</span><span class="hive-slot-name">${HiveCore.getBee(id).name}</span></button>`
```

  (b) Owned collection card — replace the owned-card art line:
```js
            <div class="hive-card-art">${beeTierEmoji(b.tier)}</div>
```
  with:
```js
            <div class="hive-card-art">${beeArt(b, "bee-art-card")}</div>
```
  (Leave the LOCKED card's `<div class="hive-card-art">❓</div>` unchanged — undiscovered bees never reveal art.)

  In `_hiveShowDetail`, replace the detail art line:
```js
            <div class="hive-detail-art">${beeTierEmoji(b.tier)}</div>
```
  with:
```js
            <div class="hive-detail-art">${beeArt(b, "bee-art-detail")}</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify it parses**

Run: `node --check WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-collection.js`
Expected: no output. (With no manifest/art present, `beeArt` returns emoji — identical to current behavior — so the album is unchanged until art is generated.)

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**


## Task 5: `.bee-art-img` CSS

**Files:**
- Modify: `WordPlay/wwwroot/css/app.css`

- [ ] **Step 1: Append** to the end of `WordPlay/wwwroot/css/app.css`:

```css
/* Illustrated bee art (Hive); falls back to .bee-art-emoji when missing. */
.bee-art-img { object-fit: cover; border-radius: 50%; display: inline-block; vertical-align: middle; }
.bee-art-card { width: 46px; height: 46px; }
.bee-art-slot { width: 34px; height: 34px; }
.bee-art-detail { width: 96px; height: 96px; }
.bee-art-emoji { display: inline-block; line-height: 1; }
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify braces balanced**

Run: `node -e "const c=require('fs').readFileSync('WordPlay/wwwroot/css/app.css','utf8'); const o=(c.match(/{/g)||[]).length,x=(c.match(/}/g)||[]).length; if(o!==x)throw new Error('mismatch '+o+'/'+x); console.log('balanced',o)"`
Expected: "balanced N".

**Files:**

- Create: `test/bee-art.test.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `tools/bee-prompts.json`** with all 30 entries:

```json
{
  "worker":    { "desc": "a sturdy honeybee carrying a tiny bucket beside the honeycomb", "palette": "warm amber, honey gold, soft brown" },
  "forager":   { "desc": "a cheerful bee hugging an overflowing pollen basket in a wildflower meadow", "palette": "meadow green, pollen yellow, sky blue" },
  "gleaner":   { "desc": "a tidy little bee sweeping up stray pollen grains", "palette": "wheat gold, soft tan, cream" },
  "clover":    { "desc": "a happy bee perched on a four-leaf clover", "palette": "clover green, white, soft pink" },
  "dewdrop":   { "desc": "a tiny bee sipping a sparkling dewdrop on a misty leaf", "palette": "soft mint, dewy silver, pale gold" },
  "sprig":     { "desc": "a sprightly young bee bouncing on a fresh green sprig", "palette": "fresh green, lime, sunny yellow" },
  "pollen":    { "desc": "a fuzzy bee completely dusted in golden pollen", "palette": "golden yellow, ochre, warm cream" },
  "blossom":   { "desc": "a gentle bee nestled in the first pink blossom of spring", "palette": "blossom pink, spring green, white" },
  "scout":     { "desc": "an adventurous bee with a tiny explorer cap scanning the horizon", "palette": "sky blue, leaf green, warm tan" },
  "nurse":     { "desc": "a kindly bee with a small nurse cap tending honeycomb cells", "palette": "soft white, gentle pink, honey gold" },
  "dancer":    { "desc": "a lively bee mid waggle-dance with playful motion swirls", "palette": "vivid orange, sunflower yellow, warm brown" },
  "gardener":  { "desc": "a bee with a tiny watering can in a blooming garden", "palette": "garden green, terracotta, flower pink" },
  "thistle":   { "desc": "a hardy bee resting on a purple thistle bloom", "palette": "thistle purple, prickly green, silver" },
  "lavender":  { "desc": "a serene bee drifting over a lavender field", "palette": "lavender purple, sage green, soft gold" },
  "sunflower": { "desc": "a sunny bee atop a giant sunflower facing the sun", "palette": "sunflower yellow, leaf green, sky blue" },
  "bramble":   { "desc": "a clever bee tucked in a tangled bramble hedge with berries", "palette": "bramble green, berry red, dusky purple" },
  "drone":     { "desc": "a plump relaxed bee lounging on a cushion of honeycomb", "palette": "rich amber, deep gold, cozy brown" },
  "sentinel":  { "desc": "a watchful bee in tiny armor guarding the hive entrance", "palette": "burnished bronze, steel grey, honey gold" },
  "amber":     { "desc": "a radiant bee with translucent amber wings glowing in sunlight", "palette": "glowing amber, honey orange, warm gold" },
  "nectarine": { "desc": "a sweet bee sipping from a ripe nectarine in an orchard", "palette": "nectarine orange, blush pink, leaf green" },
  "honeydew":  { "desc": "a glistening bee coated in shimmering honeydew", "palette": "dewy gold, pale green, glassy white" },
  "dusk":      { "desc": "a calm bee foraging under a twilight sky with fireflies", "palette": "twilight indigo, dusky purple, firefly gold" },
  "marigold":  { "desc": "a vivid bee blazing atop a bright marigold", "palette": "marigold orange, deep yellow, ember red" },
  "regent":    { "desc": "a noble bee wearing a small golden circlet on a throne of comb", "palette": "royal gold, deep amber, regal crimson" },
  "warden":    { "desc": "a steadfast bee in a watchkeeper cloak holding a lantern at dawn", "palette": "dawn gold, slate blue, warm bronze" },
  "artisan":   { "desc": "a masterful bee sculpting a flawless honeycomb with tiny tools", "palette": "honey gold, warm copper, soft cream" },
  "luminary":  { "desc": "a glowing legendary bee trailing soft light over a vast meadow", "palette": "luminous gold, soft white, meadow green" },
  "monarch":   { "desc": "a majestic crowned bee with grand shimmering wings on a flowering throne", "palette": "imperial gold, royal purple, deep amber" },
  "empress":   { "desc": "a beloved empress bee radiant amid a complete thriving hive", "palette": "radiant gold, rose, warm ivory" },
  "solstice":  { "desc": "an ancient golden bee haloed by the midsummer sun", "palette": "blazing gold, sunrise orange, warm white" }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write the drift test** — create `test/bee-art.test.js`:

```js
const test = require("node:test");
const assert = require("node:assert");
const core = require("../WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-core.js");
const prompts = require("../tools/bee-prompts.json");

test("every registry bee has a prompt, and every prompt maps to a bee", () => {
  const ids = core.BEES.map(b => b.id).sort();
  const keys = Object.keys(prompts).sort();
  assert.deepStrictEqual(keys, ids);
});

test("every prompt has a non-empty desc and palette", () => {
  for (const k of Object.keys(prompts)) {
    assert.ok(prompts[k].desc && prompts[k].desc.length > 0, k + " desc");
    assert.ok(prompts[k].palette && prompts[k].palette.length > 0, k + " palette");
  }
});
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Run to verify pass**

Run: `node --test test/bee-art.test.js`
Expected: PASS (2 tests). Then `npm test` — all green.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add tools/bee-prompts.json test/bee-art.test.js
git commit -m "feat: bee art prompts and registry-prompt drift test"
```

---

## Task 7: `tools/generate-bees.js`

**Files:**
- Create: `tools/generate-bees.js`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `tools/generate-bees.js`:**

```js
#!/usr/bin/env node
// ============================================================
// WordPlay — Bee Art Batch Generator
// Uses Flux 2 Pro on fal.ai to generate cute cartoon mascot bees.
// Clone of generate-backgrounds.js. Setup: tools/.env with FAL_KEY=...
// Usage:
//   node tools/generate-bees.js              # generate all missing
//   node tools/generate-bees.js --dry-run    # preview prompts
//   node tools/generate-bees.js --only "worker,monarch"
//   node tools/generate-bees.js --manifest   # rebuild manifest only
// ============================================================
const fs = require("fs");
const path = require("path");
const sharp = require("sharp");
require("dotenv").config({ path: path.join(__dirname, ".env") });
const { fal } = require("@fal-ai/client");

const PROMPTS_FILE = path.join(__dirname, "bee-prompts.json");
const OUTPUT_DIR = path.join(__dirname, "..", "WordPlay", "wwwroot", "images", "bees");
const MANIFEST_FILE = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "bees-manifest.json");
const WEBP_QUALITY = 82;
const IMAGE_SIZE = 384;
const DELAY_MS = 2500;

const PROMPT_TEMPLATE = (desc, palette) =>
    `A cute cartoon mascot bee character, ${desc}, big friendly eyes, soft rounded shapes, ` +
    `${palette} color palette, centered in a small circular vignette of its habitat, ` +
    `charming children's mobile-game collectible sticker illustration, soft clean lighting, no text`;

function sleep(ms) { return new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms)); }

async function downloadAndConvert(url, outputPath) {
    const resp = await fetch(url);
    if (!resp.ok) throw new Error(`Download failed: ${resp.status}`);
    const buffer = Buffer.from(await resp.arrayBuffer());
    await sharp(buffer).resize(IMAGE_SIZE, IMAGE_SIZE, { fit: "cover" }).webp({ quality: WEBP_QUALITY }).toFile(outputPath);
}

function buildManifest() {
    const files = fs.readdirSync(OUTPUT_DIR).filter((f) => f.endsWith(".webp"));
    const ids = files.map((f) => f.replace(/\.webp$/, ""));
    fs.writeFileSync(MANIFEST_FILE, JSON.stringify(ids, null, 2));
    console.log(`Manifest written: ${ids.length} bees`);
    return ids;
}

async function main() {
    const args = process.argv.slice(2);
    const dryRun = args.includes("--dry-run");
    const manifestOnly = args.includes("--manifest");
    let onlyKeys = null;
    const onlyIdx = args.indexOf("--only");
    if (onlyIdx !== -1 && args[onlyIdx + 1]) onlyKeys = new Set(args[onlyIdx + 1].split(",").map((s) => s.trim()));

    fs.mkdirSync(OUTPUT_DIR, { recursive: true });
    if (manifestOnly) { buildManifest(); return; }

    const prompts = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(PROMPTS_FILE, "utf8"));
    const allKeys = Object.keys(prompts);
    const keys = onlyKeys ? allKeys.filter((k) => onlyKeys.has(k)) : allKeys;

    if (!dryRun && !process.env.FAL_KEY) {
        console.error("Error: FAL_KEY not set in tools/.env");
        process.exit(1);
    }
    if (!dryRun) fal.config({ credentials: process.env.FAL_KEY });

    const todo = [];
    let skipped = 0;
    for (const key of keys) {
        if (fs.existsSync(path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, key + ".webp"))) { skipped++; continue; }
        todo.push(key);
    }
    console.log(`Total: ${keys.length}  Exist: ${skipped}  To generate: ${todo.length}`);
    console.log(`Est. cost: ~$${(todo.length * 0.03).toFixed(2)}\n`);

    if (dryRun) {
        for (const key of todo.slice(0, 5)) {
            console.log(`  ${key}:\n    ${PROMPT_TEMPLATE(prompts[key].desc, prompts[key].palette)}\n`);
        }
        return;
    }
    if (todo.length === 0) { console.log("Nothing to generate."); buildManifest(); return; }

    let done = 0, errors = 0;
    for (const key of todo) {
        done++;
        const entry = prompts[key];
        const prompt = PROMPT_TEMPLATE(entry.desc, entry.palette);
        const outputPath = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, key + ".webp");
        process.stdout.write(`[${done}/${todo.length}] ${key} ... `);
        try {
            const result = await fal.subscribe("fal-ai/flux-2-pro", {
                input: {
                    prompt,
                    image_size: { width: IMAGE_SIZE, height: IMAGE_SIZE },
                    num_images: 1,
                    safety_tolerance: "5",
                },
            });
            const url = result.data.images[0].url;
            await downloadAndConvert(url, outputPath);
            console.log("ok");
        } catch (e) {
            errors++;
            console.log("ERROR: " + e.message);
        }
        if (done < todo.length) await sleep(DELAY_MS);
    }
    console.log(`\nDone. ${done - errors} generated, ${errors} errors.`);
    buildManifest();
}

main();
```

> Note: the `fal.subscribe` input + `result.data.images[0].url` extraction match `generate-backgrounds.js` verbatim (including `num_images: 1` and `safety_tolerance: "5"`).

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify it parses + dry-run works** (no API key needed for dry-run):

Run: `node tools/generate-bees.js --dry-run`
Expected: prints "Total: 30 ... To generate: 30" and 5 sample prompts. (If `dotenv`/`@fal-ai/client` aren't installed locally, run `npm install` first — they are already in package.json deps.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add tools/generate-bees.js
git commit -m "feat: bee art generator tool"
```

## Task 9: Generate the art (USER-RUN)

> This is the one step requiring the user's fal.ai API key. The agent should PAUSE here and ask the user to run it, then resume to commit the output. Do not attempt to run it without confirmation that `FAL_KEY` is set and the user approves the ~$1 spend.

- [ ] **Step 1: User runs the generator**

Run (user, with `tools/.env` containing `FAL_KEY=...`): `node tools/generate-bees.js`
Expected: `[1/30] worker ... ok` … through 30, then "Manifest written: 30 bees". ~$1, a few minutes.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify output**

Run: `node -e "const m=require('./WordPlay/wwwroot/images/bees/bees-manifest.json'); console.log(m.length, 'bees'); const fs=require('fs'); console.log(fs.readdirSync('WordPlay/wwwroot/images/bees').filter(f=>f.endsWith('.webp')).length, 'webps')"`
Expected: `30 bees` and `30 webps`.

Run the drift/coverage check: `node -e "const m=new Set(require('./WordPlay/wwwroot/images/bees/bees-manifest.json')); const core=require('./WordPlay/wwwroot/js/bee-core.js'); const missing=core.BEES.map(b=>b.id).filter(id=>!m.has(id)); console.log('missing art:', missing.length ? missing : 'none')"`
Expected: `missing art: none`.


## Notes / deferred

- Per-bee animation, shiny variants, seasonal bees — out of scope (YAGNI).
- If fal Flux output framing is inconsistent (bee off-center / not circular), tune the `PROMPT_TEMPLATE` wording in `generate-bees.js` and regenerate with `--only` for the affected ids; the manifest/album handle partial regeneration gracefully.
- Optional later: add `images/bees/` to the SW fetch handler as a runtime cache-first route for offline album art (not required for v1; browser HTTP cache suffices).
